user_query:
I need a consolidated bug list grouped by status for a specific project from my local bug tracker. Do this:

1) Read input/config.json to get an optional project_id filter.
2) Fetch bugs with a GET request to http://localhost:3456/api/bugs. If project_id is present in config, append ?project_id=<value>.
3) If the server request fails or is unreachable, fall back to reading input/fixture_bugs.json and use its "data" array as the bug list (still apply the same project_id filter when present).
4) Save the filtered bug list (just the array of bug objects, no wrapper) to output/raw/bugs.json. Create the output/raw/ directory if it doesn’t exist.
5) Write a markdown report to output/bugs_report.md grouped by status with three sections in this exact order: "## open", "## fixed", "## closed". Under each section, include a markdown table with columns exactly: id | title | priority | created_at. Within each section, sort rows by priority (high > medium > low), then by created_at ascending.
6) Write a CSV summary to output/bugs_summary.csv with header "status,count" and rows for "open", "fixed", and "closed" in that order, plus a final row "total,<sum>".
7) Write output/metadata.json capturing: { "source_endpoint": "http://localhost:3456/api/bugs" (with ?project_id=... when used), "project_filter": <string or null>, and "generated_at": an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp (e.g., 2026-04-14T12:34:56Z) }.

Make sure the report strictly uses the exact section headings and table columns specified above.
